title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A portrait of the Jazz Age in all of its decadence and excess, exploring the tragic story of Jay Gatsby and his obsession with Daisy Buchanan.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: The unforgettable novel of a childhood in a sleepy Southern town and the crisis of conscience that rocked it amidst racial injustice.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling dystopian prophecy depicting a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother, continuous surveillance, and the suppression of truth.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: An exploration of how Homo sapiens conquered the Earth, established civilizations, and reshaped our biology and collective belief systems.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: A tour of the mind explaining the two systems that drive the way we think: System 1 is fast and intuitive, System 2 is slow and deliberate.

title: The Power of Habit
author: Charles Duhigg
description: An investigation into why habits exist and how they can be changed, combining scientific research with compelling real-world stories.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: The spirited Elizabeth Bennet clashes with the proud Mr. Darcy in Austen's witty masterpiece of love, family, and social expectations.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: An inspiring allegorical novel about a young Andalusian shepherd journeying to the pyramids to fulfill his personal legend.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: The landmark chronicle of the Buendía family across seven generations in the mythical Colombian town of Macondo.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: On the occasion of his fifth wedding anniversary, Nick Dunne reports that his beautiful wife Amy has disappeared under suspicious circumstances.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
description: Symbologist Robert Langdon investigates a murder in the Louvre museum, uncovering a centuries-old conspiracy involving secret societies.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A famous painter shoots her husband five times and then never speaks another word, challenging a criminal psychotherapist obsessed with uncovering the truth.

title: People We Meet on Vacation
author: Emily Henry
description: Two estranged best friends who used to take an annual summer vacation together attempt to mend their relationship on one final trip to Palm Springs.

title: Me Before You
author: Jojo Moyes
description: Louisa Clark becomes a caregiver for Will Traynor, a wealthy banker left paralyzed after an accident, forming an emotional bond that changes both their lives.

title: The Notebook
author: Nicholas Sparks
description: An elderly man reads a faded notebook to a fellow nursing home resident, retelling the story of Noah and Allie's enduring summer romance.

title: The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Frodo Baggins sets out on a perilous quest from the Shire across Middle-earth to destroy the One Ring in the fires of Mount Doom.

title: A Game of Thrones
author: George R.R. Martin
description: In a land where summers span decades and winters can last a lifetime, noble families battle for the Iron Throne of Westeros.

title: The Way of Kings
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: The epic fantasy opener of the Stormlight Archive set on Roshar, a tempest-swept world where knights bound to ancient armor once stood as humanity's guardians.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: Set on the desert planet Arrakis, young Paul Atreides navigates political intrigue, ecological survival, and destiny surrounding the most valuable substance in the universe.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: Psychohistorian Hari Seldon foresees the fall of the Galactic Empire and establishes two foundations of scientists and scholars to shorten the ensuing dark age.

title: Neuromancer
author: William Gibson
description: The seminal cyberpunk novel following washed-up computer hacker Case as he is hired for a heist involving powerful corporate artificial intelligences.

title: The Shining
author: Stephen King
description: Jack Torrance takes a winter caretaker position at the isolated Overlook Hotel with his family, where supernatural forces prey upon his sanity and his son's psychic gifts.

title: Frankenstein
author: Mary Shelley
description: Scientist Victor Frankenstein animates a creature created from reassembled body parts, unleashing tragic consequences of unchecked ambition and social alienation.

title: Dracula
author: Bram Stoker
description: Count Dracula attempts to move from Transylvania to England so that he may find new blood and spread the undead curse, while Professor Van Helsing leads the resistance.

title: Treasure Island
author: Robert Louis Stevenson
description: Young Jim Hawkins discovers a treasure map and embarks on a dangerous sea voyage aboard the Hispaniola alongside the cunning pirate Long John Silver.

title: The Count of Monte Cristo
author: Alexandre Dumas
description: Wrongfully imprisoned for fourteen years, Edmond Dantes escapes from the Chateau d'If, unearths a vast hidden fortune, and enacts meticulous retribution against his betrayers.

title: The Call of the Wild
author: Jack London
description: Buck, a domesticated dog, is stolen from his California home and sold as a sled dog in the brutal Klondike Gold Rush, discovering his primal survival instincts.

title: The Nightingale
author: Kristin Hannah
description: Two French sisters in German-occupied France during World War II embark on dangerous paths toward survival, love, and heroism under brutal oppression.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
description: A blind French girl and a German orphan boy whose paths collide in occupied Saint-Malo as both try to survive the devastation of World War II.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: In the theocratic republic of Gilead, Offred must navigate strict societal roles and fertility servitude while remembering the lost liberties of the United States.

title: Steve Jobs
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The exclusive biography based on over forty interviews with Apple co-founder Steve Jobs along with hundreds of conversations with family, competitors, and colleagues.

title: Titan: The Life of John D. Rockefeller
author: Ron Chernow
description: A monumental biography of America's first billionaire and Standard Oil founder, capturing both his ruthless business dominance and his extraordinary philanthropic legacy.

title: Alexander Hamilton
author: Ron Chernow
description: The riveting chronicle of the orphan immigrant who became George Washington's right-hand man, architect of the American financial system, and Founding Father.

title: Long Walk to Freedom
author: Nelson Mandela
description: The autobiography of Nelson Mandela, recounting his early life, twenty-seven years of political imprisonment, and his triumph over apartheid as South Africa's president.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
author: Anne Frank
description: The moving diary kept by Anne Frank while hiding with her family in a secret annex in Amsterdam during the Nazi occupation of the Netherlands.

title: Wings of Fire: An Autobiography
author: A.P.J. Abdul Kalam
description: The inspiring life story of Dr. Kalam from his humble upbringing in Rameswaram to leading India's space and missile defense programs and serving as President.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: An actionable framework demonstrating how tiny 1% daily changes can compound into remarkable life breakthroughs in productivity and personal health.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
author: Dale Carnegie
description: The foundational self-help classic detailing fundamental principles for handling people, building trust, resolving disagreements, and becoming a charismatic communicator.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
description: A counterintuitive guide to living a good life by embracing limitations, choosing meaningful values, and letting go of superficial positivity.

title: The Hard Thing About Hard Things
author: Ben Horowitz
description: Practical, brutal advice on building and running a startup, covering the grueling decisions when there are no easy answers during corporate crises.

title: The Intelligent Investor
author: Benjamin Graham
description: The definitive treatise on value investing, providing timeless guidance on risk management, margin of safety, and emotional discipline in financial markets.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: How continuous innovation, validated learning, and rapid experimentation create radically successful product companies and reduce startup capital waste.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
author: Viktor E. Frankl
description: Psychiatrist Viktor Frankl's harrowing experience in Nazi concentration camps and his introduction of logotherapy, arguing that human purpose is found through meaning.

title: Influence: The Psychology of Persuasion
author: Robert B. Cialdini
description: A comprehensive examination of the six psychological levers of persuasion: reciprocity, commitment, social proof, authority, liking, and scarcity.

title: The Body Keeps the Score
author: Bessel van der Kolk
description: A pioneering psychiatrist explores how psychological trauma physically alters neural pathways in the brain and presents new paths to healing and recovery.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
description: The private reflections and stoic philosophical journal of Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius on self-discipline, resilience, virtue, and emotional calm.

title: The Republic
author: Plato
description: Socrates dialogues on justice, the structure of the ideal city-state, the allegory of the cave, and the education of philosopher-kings.

title: Beyond Good and Evil
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
description: Nietzsche challenges traditional Christian dogmatic morality, introducing the concepts of the will to power, master-slave morality, and perspective inquiry.

title: The Bhagavad Gita
author: Vyasa (Translated by Eknath Easwaran)
description: Lord Krishna imparts spiritual guidance to Arjuna on the battlefield of Kurukshetra regarding duty (Dharma), selfless action (Karma Yoga), and divine devotion.

title: Tao Te Ching
author: Laozi
description: The foundational scripture of Taoism presenting poetic aphorisms on natural balance, humility, simplicity, and effortless action (Wu Wei).

title: The Power of Now
author: Eckhart Tolle
description: A spiritual guide illustrating how living entirely in the present moment frees the conscious mind from anxiety and egoic overthinking.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Cosmologist Stephen Hawking makes fundamental questions regarding black holes, quantum mechanics, spacetime curvature, and the origin of the universe accessible.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A breathtaking synthesis of science, philosophy, and planetary exploration examining fourteen billion years of cosmic evolution and humanity's place within it.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A revolutionary perspective on Darwinian evolution explaining natural selection from the viewpoint of the gene as the fundamental unit of replication.

title: The Innovators
author: Walter Isaacson
description: The collaborative history of the programmers, engineers, and visionaries who created the computer and the internet, from Ada Lovelace to Alan Turing and Bill Gates.

title: The Master Switch
author: Tim Wu
description: An analysis of the historical cycles of information empires, tracing how open communication networks inevitably face corporate consolidation and regulatory control.

title: Life 3.0: Being Human in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
author: Max Tegmark
description: MIT physicist Max Tegmark explores how superintelligent machines will impact the job market, military warfare, cosmic colonization, and human consciousness.

title: The C Programming Language
author: Brian W. Kernighan & Dennis M. Ritchie
description: The legendary guide written by the original designers of the C language at Bell Labs, teaching fundamental data structures, memory pointers, and systems programming.

title: Clean Code: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship
author: Robert C. Martin
description: Practical principles and case studies demonstrating how to write readable, maintainable, and testable software architecture while avoiding code smells.

title: Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software
author: Erich Gamma, Richard Helm, Ralph Johnson, John Vlissides
description: The classic 'Gang of Four' reference cataloging twenty-three fundamental object-oriented software design patterns to solve recurring architectural challenges.

title: Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach
author: Stuart Russell & Peter Norvig
description: The standard university textbook covering intelligent agents, search algorithms, probabilistic reasoning, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and robotics.

title: Deep Learning
author: Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, Aaron Courville
description: The authoritative comprehensive textbook detailing mathematical foundations of neural networks, optimization algorithms, convolutional networks, and generative modeling.

title: Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras, and TensorFlow
author: Aurélien Géron
description: An intuitive, practical engineering guide demonstrating how to build end-to-end intelligent systems and train deep neural networks using Python libraries.

title: Python for Data Analysis
author: Wes McKinney
description: Written by the creator of the pandas library, this guide covers data wrangling, time series manipulation, vector operations, and visualization with NumPy and IPython.

title: The Elements of Statistical Learning
author: Trevor Hastie, Robert Tibshirani, Jerome Friedman
description: The advanced mathematical foundation for statistical modeling, supervised classification, boosting methods, kernel methods, and high-dimensional regression.

title: Data Science from Scratch
author: Joel Grus
description: Learn the fundamental mathematical principles and algorithmic machinery of data science by implementing linear algebra and machine learning models from scratch in Python.

title: The Princeton Companion to Mathematics
author: Timothy Gowers (Editor)
description: An exhaustive, accessible survey of pure mathematics featuring over two hundred articles explaining major theorems, number theory, geometry, and modern mathematical research.

title: A Book of Abstract Algebra
author: Charles C. Pinter
description: An introductory textbook presenting group theory, rings, integral domains, vector spaces, and Galois theory with intuitive examples and historical context.

title: Linear Algebra Done Right
author: Sheldon Axler
description: A rigorous undergraduate text that takes a determinant-free approach to linear algebra, focusing directly on vector spaces and linear operators.

title: Engineering Mechanics: Dynamics
author: Russell C. Hibbeler
description: The gold standard engineering reference covering particle kinematics, force acceleration, rigid-body planar dynamics, and mechanical vibration analysis.

title: Shigley's Mechanical Engineering Design
author: Richard G. Budynas & Keith J. Nisbett
description: Comprehensive engineering design principles detailing material stress concentration, fatigue failure theories, gear systems, bearings, and mechanical linkages.

title: Introduction to Electric Circuits
author: Richard C. Dorf & James A. Svoboda
description: An analysis of DC and AC circuit theory, nodal and mesh analysis, operational amplifiers, Laplace transforms, and frequency response modeling.

title: Gray's Anatomy for Students
author: Richard L. Drake, A. Wayne Vogl, Adam W. M. Mitchell
description: An acclaimed, clinically oriented human anatomy reference featuring stunning anatomical illustrations and diagnostic imaging correlation for medical students.

title: Harrison's Principles of Internal Medicine
author: J. Larry Jameson & Anthony S. Fauci
description: The premier clinical diagnostic encyclopedia covering pathophysiology, clinical immunology, infectious diseases, cardiology, and oncology.

title: Robbins and Cotran Pathologic Basis of Disease
author: Vinay Kumar, Abul K. Abbas, Jon C. Aster
description: The definitive textbook on human disease mechanisms, cellular injury, inflammation, genetic pathology, and organ system pathogenesis.

title: Black's Law Dictionary
author: Bryan A. Garner (Editor-in-Chief)
description: The most widely cited legal dictionary in American jurisprudence, defining over fifty thousand legal terms, precedents, constitutional doctrine, and statutory phrasing.

title: A Theory of Justice
author: John Rawls
description: John Rawls presents the 'veil of ignorance' thought experiment, arguing for constitutional justice as fairness and equal distribution of basic civil liberties.

title: The Concept of Law
author: H.L.A. Hart
description: A classic of legal positivism analyzing the structure of legal rules, the separation of law and morality, and the nature of judicial obligation.

title: Indian Polity for Civil Services Examinations
author: M. Laxmikanth
description: The indispensable reference for UPSC civil service aspirants covering the Indian Constitution, parliamentary structure, fundamental rights, and federal governance.

title: India's Struggle for Independence
author: Bipan Chandra
description: A comprehensive historical chronicle of the Indian freedom movement from the 1857 revolt through the Gandhian era to independence in 1947.

title: A Brief History of Modern India
author: Rajiv Ahir (Spectrum)
description: Concise historical analysis tailored for competitive examinations detailing British colonial administration, socio-religious reforms, and nationalist resistance.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Orphaned Harry discovers on his eleventh birthday that he is a wizard and begins his education at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry.

title: Charlotte's Web
author: E.B. White
description: The tender tale of a barnyard pig named Wilbur and his extraordinary friendship with a wise, articulate spider named Charlotte.

title: The Little Prince
author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
description: A stranded aviator in the Sahara Desert meets a young prince visiting from Asteroid B-612, learning profound lessons about love, friendship, and human folly.

title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In the ruins of North America, sixteen-year-old Katniss Everdeen volunteers to replace her younger sister in a televised battle to the death.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
author: John Green
description: Two teenage cancer patients meet in a support group and embark on a poignant, humorous, and heartbreaking journey to visit a reclusive author in Amsterdam.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
description: Narrated by Death during World War II Germany, young Liesel Meminger steals books from book burnings and shares them with the Jewish refugee hidden in her basement.

title: Maus I: A Survivor's Tale
author: Art Spiegelman
description: A Pulitzer Prize-winning graphic novel depicting the Holocaust using animals metaphorically—Jews as mice and Nazis as cats—through the author's father's testimony.

title: Watchmen
author: Alan Moore & Dave Gibbons
description: In an alternate Cold War 1985 where masked vigilantes are outlawed, the murder of a former teammate uncovers a terrifying global conspiracy.

title: Persepolis: The Story of a Childhood
author: Marjane Satrapi
description: The autobiographical memoir of a young girl growing up in Tehran during the Islamic Revolution and the devastating Iran-Iraq War.

title: The Odyssey
author: Homer (Translated by Robert Fagles)
description: The epic Greek poem chronicling the ten-year voyage of King Odysseus returning home to Ithaca after the Trojan War while facing mythical monsters.

title: The Divine Comedy
author: Dante Alighieri
description: Guided by Virgil and Beatrice, Dante undertakes an allegorical journey through Inferno, Purgatorio, and Paradiso, exploring divine justice and the human soul.

title: Leaves of Grass
author: Walt Whitman
description: A celebration of democracy, nature, physical vitality, and individual freedom written in ecstatic free verse by America's premier national poet.

title: Great Expectations
author: Charles Dickens
description: Orphan Pip comes into an unexpected fortune from a mysterious benefactor while entangled with the eccentric Miss Havisham and her haughty ward Estella.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Impoverished student Raskolnikov murders an unscrupulous pawnbroker to test his philosophical superiority, only to be pursued by relentless psychological guilt.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: The tragic affair between aristocrat Anna Karenina and Count Vronsky unfolds against the backdrop of 19th-century Russian societal hypocrisy and rural reform.

title: Why We Sleep: Unlocking the Power of Sleep and Dreams
author: Matthew Walker
description: Neuroscientist Matthew Walker reveals how sleep strengthens immunity, enhances memory, regulates hormone balance, and increases overall lifespan.

title: Born to Run
author: Christopher McDougall
description: An expedition into Mexico's Copper Canyons to study the Tarahumara tribe, whose legendary barefoot ultra-running capabilities challenge biomechanical norms.

title: The Obesity Code
author: Jason Fung
description: Dr. Jason Fung explains how insulin regulation and intermittent fasting hold the true key to sustainable weight loss and metabolic restoration.

title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
author: Samin Nosrat
description: A revolutionary culinary masterclass teaching the four basic elements that determine flavor harmony and cooking success across all world kitchens.

title: Kitchen Confidential: Adventures in the Culinary Underbelly
author: Anthony Bourdain
description: Chef Anthony Bourdain pulls back the restaurant curtain with raw, hilarious, and chaotic memoirs of life behind professional kitchen line stations.

title: The Food Lab: Better Home Cooking Through Science
author: J. Kenji López-Alt
description: An encyclopedia explaining the thermodynamics and molecular biology of heat transfer, emulsions, and searing techniques to perfect classic recipes.

title: In a Sunburned Country
author: Bill Bryson
description: Bill Bryson's witty, adventurous travelogue across Australia celebrating its unique geography, extreme wildlife, friendly locals, and vast outback history.

title: Eat, Pray, Love
author: Elizabeth Gilbert
description: A woman embarks on a year-long quest of self-discovery, eating in Italy, experiencing meditation in India, and finding inner peace in Bali.

title: On the Road
author: Jack Kerouac
description: The quintessential Beat Generation road novel chronicling Sal Paradise and Neal Cassady's cross-country American journeys powered by jazz and freedom.

title: Ways of Seeing
author: John Berger
description: Based on the BBC television series, John Berger deconstructs how western oil painting, advertising, and cultural politics shape visual perception.

title: The Design of Everyday Things
author: Don Norman
description: The primer on human-centered usability, explaining affordances, signifiers, intuitive feedback, and why poor user interface design causes human errors.

title: The Story of Art
author: E.H. Gombrich
description: The most famous and accessible introduction to global art history ever published, spanning cave paintings through the Renaissance to modern artistic movements.
